qa_pairs.question: What are LAMMPS packages, and how do they affect the program?
qa_pairs.answer: LAMMPS packages are components that can be enabled or disabled when compiling the program. Recent versions of LAMMPS contain about 60 different packages, but not all can be enabled in a single executable. The specific packages included are what differentiate the various LAMMPS modules available.

qa_pairs.question: Why might a LAMMPS simulation fail even after loading a particular module?
qa_pairs.answer: A LAMMPS simulation might fail if a necessary package was not enabled during the compilation of the specific module being used. Not all of the approximately 60 available packages can be enabled in a single executable.
